signals.headline: Details:** Statistics Korea confirmed the data today, showing headline CPI at 2
signals.raw: Details:** Statistics Korea confirmed the data today, showing headline CPI at 2.2% YoY and 0.3% MoM (vs 0.6% expected), with core CPI at 2.2% YoY (down slightly from Feb).[1][3] Oil/transport prices rose sharply (+5% YoY), offset by falling food (+0.5% YoY) and policy interventions; Bank of Korea likely holds rates at 2.5% in April.[1][2] This softer-than-expected print amid energy shocks could sh

signals.headline: ETH Price Rally on Pectra Upgrade Final Testing & ETF Inflows | YES | HIGH confidence**
signals.raw:
ETH Price Rally on Pectra Upgrade Final Testing & ETF Inflows | YES | HIGH confidence**  
Ethereum is surging as of April 18, 2026, driven by the **Pectra (Prague-Electra) Upgrade** entering its final testing phase, boosting wallet abstraction and validator efficiency, alongside record Spot Ethereum ETF inflows and Layer 2 TVL milestones like Base and Arbitrum.[3]

signals.headline: South Korea's March 2026 CPI inflation rose to 2
signals.raw: South Korea's March 2026 CPI inflation rose to 2.2% YoY (from 2.0% in Feb), below 2.3% consensus, driven by oil but curbed by government measures like fuel caps.**[1][3]
matched_markets: Will South Korea’s 2026 Annual Inflation be between 2.7% and 2.9%?

signals.headline: Iran re-closes Strait of Hormuz in response to ongoing US blockade | YES | HIGH confidence** [1][4][5][6]
signals.raw:
Iran re-closes Strait of Hormuz in response to ongoing US blockade | YES | HIGH confidence** [1][4][5][6]

Iran announced the re-closure of the Strait of Hormuz early Saturday, April 18, 2026, reversing Friday's reopening to commercial vessels, due to President Trump's insistence on maintaining the US blockade of Iranian ships and ports until a nuclear deal is reached.[1][5][6] Iranian officials,
